title: Research and Development in Biotechnology (except Nanobiotechnology)

narrative.executive_view: The roll-up-relevant part of this heterogeneous code is a fee-for-service preclinical contract-research laboratory, not a venture-funded drug developer. AI can remove meaningful documentation, informatics and coordination work, but its savings are diluted by hands-on assay, animal and laboratory work. Sponsor purchasing power, FTE/time-and-materials billing and expensive entry pricing make this a cautious deep-dive candidate rather than an attractive roll-up thesis. [S4][S5][S6]
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: AI is already credible in drug-development workflows: FDA reports experience with more than 500 submissions containing AI components, while BCG describes drug-discovery use cases in target discovery, validation and molecular design. For a preclinical CRO, that translates most directly into literature synthesis, protocol and report drafts, data analysis, bioinformatics, scheduling and quality-document assembly. It does not substitute for conducted studies, wet-lab execution, animal work, sample handling or accountable scientific review. [S2][S3][S5]
narrative.value_capture: The operator can retain savings on scoped, fixed-fee or milestone work until market prices reset, but FTE and time-and-materials arrangements sell scientist capacity and pass productivity gains to the sponsor through fewer billed hours or renegotiated rates. Grand View describes fee-for-service, time-and-materials/FTE and fixed-price/milestone structures across preclinical CRO work; the mix at a small target is not observed here. Scientific reputation, validated methods and sponsor relationships provide some differentiation, but large pharma can internalize digital discovery and still outsource only physical execution. [S2][S4]
narrative.adoption_timing: The chosen 20% current effective-adoption estimate is below the 50% boundary because public evidence demonstrates use cases and submissions, not firm-wide, labor-saving deployment across small US preclinical laboratories. BCG's 2022 account says adoption will not happen overnight, and FDA's 2025 framework shows that credibility and context-of-use controls remain central. A six-year path to 50% effective adoption is therefore a judgmental midpoint, not a measured forecast. [S2][S3]
narrative.consolidation_economics: There is a real outsourced-services market and observable financial-sponsor activity: KPMG recorded 78 biopharma-services deals in the first half of 2024 and reported selected transaction EBITDA multiples. Inotiv's 2025 update also shows that discovery, translational science and safety-assessment services remain commercial operating lines. These sources do not provide a clean $1-10M EBITDA, US NAICS 541714 buy/exit comp set; the selected multiples are deliberately conservative proxy estimates and the deterministic 599-target count is an upper bound because the code includes pre-revenue ventures. [S5][S6]
narrative.terminal_demand: Terminal demand is PRESSURED, not melting. AI may shrink billable analytical discovery tasks and reduce the number of experiments required, but regulated preclinical development still requires physical assays, samples, data generation and defensible evidence. The FDA's focus on credibility for AI-supported regulatory decisions reinforces that AI outputs are governed inputs, not a replacement for the underlying studies. The durable component belongs to laboratories concentrated in physical and regulated execution; an analytical-only discovery shop is materially more exposed. [S3][S5]
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: This is a heterogeneous code with a serious dataset mismatch: business R&D statistics and firm counts combine own-account, venture-funded biotech with external service providers, while the investment question concerns cash-flowing CROs. Market reports span global preclinical CROs and KPMG's disclosed multiples include broader biopharma services, so neither establishes a small-lab valuation. The biggest score sensitivities are the physical-work share, sponsor contract mix, time to effective adoption and the true count of EBITDA-positive targets. [S1][S4][S6]

sources.title: Business Enterprise Research and Development (BERD) Survey 2023
sources.what_it_supports: Official context for US business R&D and the fact that BERD covers businesses performing or funding R&D, rather than a census of fee-for-service CRO EBITDA targets.

sources.title: Adopting AI in Drug Discovery
sources.what_it_supports: AI use cases in drug discovery, partnership-led adoption, and the distinction between individual applications and end-to-end AI-enabled discovery.

sources.title: FDA Proposes Framework to Advance Credibility of AI Models Used for Drug and Biological Product Submissions
sources.what_it_supports: FDA's January 2025 AI guidance context, including its experience with more than 500 drug and biological product submissions containing AI components since 2016.

sources.title: Preclinical CRO Market Size and Share Report
sources.what_it_supports: Preclinical CRO service categories, pricing-model alternatives, and the importance of outsourcing, regulatory requirements and scientific complexity to the market.

sources.title: Inotiv Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year Financial Results for Fiscal 2025
sources.what_it_supports: A public preclinical-services operator's reported discovery, translational science, safety-assessment and research-model service activity.

sources.title: Biopharma Services Industry Update H1 2024
sources.what_it_supports: Biopharma-services deal activity, financial-sponsor activity and selected disclosed transaction EBITDA multiples, with broader-market limitations.

labor_share.method: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541714 ($65,745,913,502) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541714 ($77,835,466,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n_band.derivation: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $506,178 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541714); class <5: 2,793 firms, avg revenue/firm $684,545 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $107,131 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 701 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,517,970 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $394,062 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 476 firms, avg revenue/firm $5,808,891 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $909,091 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 599 firms, avg revenue/firm $18,599,482 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,910,819 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 242 firms, avg revenue/firm $94,118,694 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $14,729,576 -> out of $1-10M band; class 500+: 126 firms, avg revenue/firm $297,425,317 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $46,547,062 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 599
n_band.margin_source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: AI exposure is substantial in scientific drafting, analysis, bioinformatics and operating paperwork, but most laboratory and in-vivo delivery is physical. Fractions measure task share, not layoffs or adoption.
caveats: The supplied 5-digit role mix is a proxy and its 62.15% residual combines physical lab, quality and administrative work.
Fractions are task-replaceability judgments, not observed headcount reductions or adoption forecasts.

breakdown_by_role.rationale: Medical-science analysis, literature and drafting automate partially; experimental accountability remains human.
Code generation and bioinformatics assistance are AI-exposed, while pipeline ownership and validation remain.
Reporting and resourcing assist, but scientific program direction and sponsor accountability persist.
Operations documentation and coordination assist; client and facility leadership remains.
Biological technicians perform hands-on preparation and assays; documentation is the limited AI-exposed component.
Project planning, status reporting and contract administration are relatively automatable.
Biophysical analysis and writing assist, while experimental interpretation remains human.
Design and documentation assist but equipment integration and validation are physical.
Managerial reporting automates partially; accountable operational decisions do not.
Business-process and document work are relatively AI-exposed.
Residual includes substantial physical lab, animal-facility and EHS work alongside admin and quality documentation.

pi_dist.rationale: A mix of scoped fixed-fee work can retain savings temporarily, while FTE/time-and-materials contracts pass productivity to sponsors; 0.45 reflects that mixed mechanism.
caveats: No observed contract-type mix for US $1-10M EBITDA NAICS 541714 providers was reopened.
Global preclinical-CRO pricing categories are a disclosed proxy for the target segment.

pi_moat.rationale: Validated assays, specialized facilities and sponsor relationships resist generic-AI bypass, although digital discovery and analysis can be internalized by sponsors.
caveats: Public operator and market evidence do not measure target-specific customer concentration, assay differentiation or sponsor switching costs.

t50_years.rationale: Public evidence establishes meaningful AI use cases and regulatory activity, but not majority effective labor-saving deployment among small preclinical labs; six years is a cautious midpoint below the current-50% boundary.
caveats: BCG is a 2022 strategic publication and FDA submission experience is not a prevalence survey of small CROs.
Effective adoption is stricter than use of an AI tool or an AI-supported submission.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: A 20% estimate recognizes active use cases and FDA-visible AI activity while avoiding an unsupported claim that a majority of small laboratories have deployed effective automation.
caveats: No direct NAICS 541714 adoption census was found; this is not an observed firm-share statistic.

historical_analogs.value: AI drug-discovery applications have progressed through partnerships and bounded use cases before end-to-end workflow change.
historical_analogs.rationale: BCG's description of partnerships and differentiated use cases supports a staged-adoption analogue rather than a quantified causal forecast.
historical_analogs.plausible_range: Staged adoption; no quantitative analogue selected
caveats: This qualitative analogue is not a measured adoption curve for contract research laboratories.

owners_60plus_pct.rationale: A deliberately modest assumption is used because the official R&D source describes business R&D, not owner ages; it avoids treating workforce or founder anecdotes as an owner-age statistic.
caveats: No direct owner-age statistic for EBITDA-positive NAICS 541714 targets was reopened.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: No code-specific succession-shortage documentation was reopened, so the B uplift is not taken.
caveats: Absence of reopened documentation is not evidence that no succession issue exists.

active_consolidators.rationale: Seven is a bounded estimate of visible strategic and financial-sponsor buyers in adjacent biopharma/preclinical services, used only as B's competition penalty.
caveats: This is not a census of NAICS 541714 buyers and broader biopharma-services deal activity includes clinical, CDMO and other adjacent businesses.

buy_mult.rationale: Nine times normalized EBITDA is a conservative entry assumption below KPMG's disclosed broader-service examples, reflecting smaller-target and code-boundary uncertainty.
caveats: KPMG's disclosed examples are broader biopharma services and are not size-matched preclinical-CRO buy comps.
No direct $1-10M EBITDA NAICS 541714 transaction series was reopened.

exit_mult.rationale: Eleven and a half times is an adjacent-platform exit assumption anchored to one disclosed broader-service transaction, not a forecast of a particular exit.
caveats: The cited 11.5x example is not a preclinical-CRO, small-platform or NAICS 541714 exit comparable.
No direct small-lab exit series was reopened.

pricing_structure.value: Mixed fee-for-service, FTE/time-and-materials, and fixed-price or milestone arrangements
pricing_structure.rationale: The market source describes these alternative contract structures; their distinct pass-through behavior is used only to frame pi_dist.
pricing_structure.plausible_range: Target-specific mix; no exact share selected
caveats: The source describes the global preclinical-CRO market rather than the actual contract mix of small US targets.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: Yes. Task-level AI exposure produces a labor-saving opportunity, while the six-year t50 separately reflects slow conversion to effective deployment.

terminal_value.justification: Physical and regulated preclinical execution persists, but AI can shrink billable analytical discovery work and reduce experimental demand for analytical-heavy providers.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: No. Role mix, preclinical-CRO market/pricing, deal activity and multiples all use disclosed proxies rather than a clean NAICS 541714 small-target census.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: FTE/time-and-materials contracts risk passing AI productivity to sponsors; fixed-fee and milestone work retain savings only until competitive repricing.

dataset_mismatch.description: The deterministic code-level labor share and firm-count inputs blend venture/grant-funded biotechnology developers with fee-for-service CROs; the investment thesis is for the latter.
sensitivity: The 599 n_band estimate is an upper bound for cash-flowing targets.
The 0.9006 labor share may overstate service-operator labor intensity when receipts exclude venture or grant funding.

subsegments: fee-for-service preclinical contract research and assay laboratories
market_boundary.dominant_subsegment: fee-for-service preclinical contract research and assay laboratories
market_boundary.selection_basis: This is the most plausible EBITDA-generating and acquirable slice; it is a hypothesis, not a measured NAICS revenue split.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: A ranges from 2.50 to 1.11 before other factors, materially changing S.
V changes with the role-weighted physical-work mix; the broad residual is the largest uncertainty.
M can be near zero if entry pricing absorbs the adjacent-platform exit premium.
A smaller cash-flowing target pool reduces B; venture-company contamination is material.

reviewer_note: Independent Sol review should reopen all six sources, test the disclosed proxy boundaries and confirm that FTE/time-and-materials pass-through is counted only in C while physical/regulatory persistence is counted only in terminal value.
